origin.name: Sweden
origin.death_registration: Skatteverket (Swedish Tax Agency handles civil registration)
origin.source: FCDO Sweden travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Stockholm guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Sweden is widely available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is often simpler than full body repatriation. You will need the death certificate, cremation certificate, and urn transport documentation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Certified English translation of death certificate
special_rules: Sweden is an EU member and Hague Apostille Convention member, which simplifies document legalisation.
Death registration is handled by Skatteverket (Swedish Tax Agency), not a local registry.
The Prosecutor's Office orders post-mortem for suspicious or violent deaths.
All Swedish-language documentation requires a certified English translation for UK acceptance.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Swedish documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Stockholm can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Sweden to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Sweden. Repatriation to the UK takes 1-2 weeks. Skatteverket registration, apostille, and air cargo all handled. Contact us 24/7.
